audio.h(3) Library Functions Manual audio.h(3)

NAME

audio.h - Stub audio types for WASM builds (uses Web Audio API instead of PortAudio).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct audio_batch_info_t
Parsed audio batch packet header information. struct audio_frame_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t audio_parse_batch_header (const void *data, size_t len, audio_batch_info_t *out_batch)
asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const audio_batch_info_t *batch)
bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t sample_rate)

Detailed Description

Stub audio types for WASM builds (uses Web Audio API instead of PortAudio).

This header provides minimal audio type definitions for WASM builds. In WASM, audio is handled by Web Audio API via AudioPipeline.ts instead of PortAudio. This stub provides just enough definitions for network packet parsing to compile.

Definition in file lib/platform/wasm/stubs/audio.h.

Function Documentation

bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t sample_rate)

Definition at line 2110 of file lib/audio/audio.c.

2110                                                           {
2111   // List of commonly supported audio sample rates
2112   static const uint32_t supported_rates[] = {
2113       8000,   // Telephone quality
2114       16000,  // Wideband telephony
2115       24000,  // High quality speech
2116       32000,  // Good for video
2117       44100,  // CD quality (less common in VoIP)
2118       48000,  // Standard professional
2119       96000,  // High-end professional
2120       192000, // Ultra-high-end mastering
2121   };
2122 
2123   const size_t rate_count = sizeof(supported_rates) / sizeof(supported_rates[0]);
2124   for (size_t i = 0; i < rate_count; i++) {
2125     if (sample_rate == supported_rates[i]) {
2126       return true;
2127     }
2128   }
2129 
2130   return false;
2131 }








asciichat_error_t audio_parse_batch_header (const void *
  data, size_t len, audio_batch_info_t * out_batch)


Definition at line 2044 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2044                                                                                                         {
2045   if (!data) {
2046     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header data pointer is NULL");
2047   }
2048 
2049   if (!out_batch) {
2050     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info output pointer is NULL");
2051   }
2052 
2053   if (len < sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t)) {
2054     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header too small (len=%zu, expected=%zu)", len,
2055                      sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t));
2056   }
2057 
2058   const audio_batch_packet_t *batch_header = (const audio_batch_packet_t *)data;
2059 
2060   // Unpack network byte order values to host byte order
2061   out_batch->batch_count = ntohl(batch_header->batch_count);
2062   out_batch->total_samples = ntohl(batch_header->total_samples);
2063   out_batch->sample_rate = ntohl(batch_header->sample_rate);
2064   out_batch->channels = ntohl(batch_header->channels);
2065 
2066   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2067 }










asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const
  audio_batch_info_t * batch)


Definition at line 2069 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


2069                                                                                {
2070   if (!batch) {
2071     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info pointer is NULL");
2072   }
2073 
2074   // Validate batch_count
2075   if (batch->batch_count == 0) {
2076     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count cannot be zero");
2077   }
2078 
2079   // Check for reasonable max (256 frames per batch is very generous)
2080   if (batch->batch_count > 256) {
2081     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count too large (batch_count=%u, max=256)", batch->batch_count);
2082   }
2083 
2084   // Validate channels (1=mono, 2=stereo, max 8 for multi-channel)
2085   if (batch->channels == 0 || batch->channels > 8) {
2086     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid channel count (channels=%u, valid=1-8)", batch->channels);
2087   }
2088 
2089   // Validate sample rate
2090   if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch->sample_rate)) {
2091     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported sample rate (sample_rate=%u)", batch->sample_rate);
2092   }
2093 
2094   // Check for reasonable sample counts
2095   if (batch->total_samples == 0) {
2096     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch has zero samples");
2097   }
2098 
2099   // Each batch typically has samples_per_frame worth of samples
2100   // For 48kHz at 20ms per frame: 48000 * 0.02 = 960 samples per frame
2101   // With max 256 frames, that's up to ~245k samples per batch
2102   if (batch->total_samples > 1000000) {
2103     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch sample count suspiciously large (total_samples=%u)",
2104                      batch->total_samples);
2105   }
2106 
2107   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2108 }
